Program

ISORC 2027 Program

Explore the conference structure, keynote session, and detailed program planning for the symposium.

Program Overview

What to expect

The program will feature invited talks, paper sessions, poster presentations, doctoral symposium activities, and networking opportunities. A single-track schedule is planned to encourage broad participation across the ISORC community.

Session themes will be selected to showcase advances in real-time distributed computing, dependable systems, edge intelligence, and cyber-physical infrastructure.

Keynote

A keynote address from an esteemed researcher or industry leader will open the symposium. The keynote speaker and topic will be announced once confirmed by the program committee.

Detailed Program

Schedule highlights

The detailed program will include session times, talk titles, poster sessions, keynote events, and networking breaks.

Session Planning

Technical sessions will be organized to maximize discussion time and allow for lively Q&A after presentations.

Poster Sessions

Poster sessions will provide space for interactive discussions with authors and community members.

Doctoral Symposium

The doctoral symposium and dissertation digests will spotlight emerging research and early-career investigators.

ISORC 2027

30th IEEE International Symposium on Real-Time Distributed Computing

April 2–4, 2027 · Indian Institute of Technology Ropar, India

Host

Department of Computer Science and Engineering, IIT Ropar

Indian Institute of Technology Ropar, Punjab, India

Contact

nitin@iitrpr.ac.in

Replace this placeholder email with the official conference contact before launch.
